title: T-098 — getSystemActorConnection() helper in @monopilot/db + constant-time Bearer compare for cron (FT-038)
prompt:
# T-098 — getSystemActorConnection() helper in @monopilot/db + constant-time Bearer compare for cron (FT-038)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-038** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P1** (S effort)

## Goal
Cron paths use getSystemActorConnection + timingSafeEqual Bearer compare.

## Implementation contract
1. getSystemActorConnection sets `app.actor_type='system'` after BEGIN.
2. Bearer check uses timingSafeEqual on Buffer.from(bearer) and Buffer.from(expected).
3. Test asserts: (a) system-actor type set; (b) constant-time compare returns false on mismatched-length input without throwing.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/src/system-actor-connection.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/__tests__/system-actor-connection.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/api/cron/[name]/route.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a cron Bearer matches CRON_SECRET, when timingSafeEqual is invoked, then it returns true.
2. Given a cron Bearer of different length, when compared, then the compare returns false without throwing.
3. Given a system-actor connection, when introspected, then `app.actor_type` is 'system'.

## Test strategy
- RED: write system-actor-connection.test.ts asserting both ACs before implementation.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test system-actor-connection`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not use === on the Bearer (timing-attack).
- Do not log the CRON_SECRET.
- Do not use raw new pg.Pool.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Add getSystemActorConnection() helper for cron paths + constant-time Bearer comparison to prevent timing attacks on cron auth.
pipeline_inputs.details: T-034 cron paths today use direct pool access and a non-constant-time string compare on the Bearer. Add a tagged system-actor connection (logs `app.actor_type=system`) and switch comparisons to crypto.timingSafeEqual.
out_of_scope: Do not change cron schedule definitions.
acceptance_criteria: Given a cron Bearer matches CRON_SECRET, when timingSafeEqual is invoked, then it returns true.
Given a cron Bearer of different length, when compared, then the compare returns false without throwing.
Given a system-actor connection, when introspected, then `app.actor_type` is 'system'.

Do not use raw new pg.Pool.
